steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$|x+4| + 4 = |2x-17| + 4$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is 4.333,13/3.

steps.title: Simplify and split into cases
steps.content: Subtracting 4 from both sides reduces the equation to $|x+4| = |2x-17|$. Since $|A| = |B|$ implies $A = B$ or $A = -B$, this absolute-value equation splits into two linear cases.

steps.title: Verify both candidates in the original equation
steps.content:
For absolute-value equations every candidate must be checked.
At $x = 21$: $|21+4| = 25$ and $|2(21)-17| = |25| = 25$. Valid.
At $x = \tfrac{13}{3}$: $\left|\tfrac{13}{3}+4\right| = \tfrac{25}{3}$ and $\left|2\cdot\tfrac{13}{3}-17\right| = \left|-\tfrac{25}{3}\right| = \tfrac{25}{3}$. Valid.
